description_long: This rifle features a robust, tactical design with a multi-barrel rotary system reminiscent of a minigun or Gatling-style firearm. The main body is constructed from dark, matte-finished metal, with visible mechanical components such as a cylindrical housing, a prominent barrel cluster, and a side-mounted box likely serving as the magazine or ammunition feed. The top of the rifle includes a simple iron sight, while the rear section is compact and lacks a traditional shoulder stock, suggesting a design optimized for mounting or vehicle use. The front end is equipped with a muzzle brake, and the overall build is heavy-duty, with reinforced elements and exposed mechanical linkages. The rifle's color palette is predominantly black with subtle metallic highlights and a few red accent details on the sight and controls.
description: A heavy, multi-barrel rotary rifle with a matte black finish, exposed mechanical parts, and a compact, stockless rear. Features a muzzle brake and iron sight.
description_view_0: Front view shows the circular arrangement of the barrels and the iron sight above, emphasizing the rotary mechanism.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the full length of the rifle, the multi-barrel cluster, side-mounted box, and the compact rear section.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the back of the rotary system and the absence of a traditional stock, with the sight visible on top.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the barrel cluster, side box, and mechanical details.
